summary:
The ideal anti-rotation feature involves offsets up to approximately 2mm; this dimension supports high levels of precision critical across multiple surgeries like reverse shoulder arthroplasties and nasal procedures without causing excessive rotational movement or complications.
To address your request more succinctly:

The best anti-rotational design uses an offset ≤2mm as it ensures necessary precision while preventing significant rotation issues seen in complex orthopedic and reconstructive surgeries such as reverse shoulder replacements and nose jobs. This small margin enhances procedural safety and effectiveness. To refine even further,

An appropriate answer emphasizing core aspects could be: 

Optimally designed blades limit their offset to around 2mm to ensure sufficient stability during delicate operations common in joint reconstructions and facial plastic surgery, thereby reducing risk from unintended movements but still allowing adequate maneuverability.
